response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer, but the profile fundamentally misaligns with this embedded networking security role. There is no indication of native-level Japanese proficiency, which is a hard requirement. C experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, with no evidence of Linux kernel analysis or modification, and no exposure to the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or L2/L3 switch development.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software development career across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Some C/C++ experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms, audio systems on Linux)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing culture, and open-source contributions
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency indicated — a mandatory requirement for the role
No demonstrated Linux kernel investigation/modification or embedded networking (L2/L3, switch, MACsec, 802.1X) experience
Recent experience is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js), far removed from firmware-level C development

key_factors.factor: General seniority and solid engineering practices
